items.title: India's GDP Growth Reaches 7.8% in FY 2025-26, Driven by Manufacturing and Services
items.whyInNews: The National Statistical Office (NSO) released the provisional estimates for India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) for the fiscal year 2025-26 on April 17, 2026, showing a robust growth of 7.8%.
items.summary: India's economy has demonstrated strong performance in the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026, with a GDP growth rate of 7.8%. This growth was primarily fueled by significant contributions from the manufacturing sector, which grew by 9.5%, and the services sector, which expanded by 8.2%. Agriculture sector growth stood at a moderate 3.1%.
keyPoints: India's GDP grew by 7.8% in FY 2025-26.
Manufacturing sector growth was 9.5%.
Services sector growth was 8.2%.
The NSO released provisional GDP estimates on April 17, 2026.
importantPoints: GDP is the total monetary or market value of all the finished goods and services produced within a country's borders in a specific time period.
The NSO is part of the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation.
Key components of GDP include consumption, investment, government spending, and net exports.
India's fiscal year runs from April 1 to March 31.
Previous fiscal year (FY 2024-25) saw a GDP growth of 7.0%.
items.examRelevance: Economy section of SSC exams; understanding economic indicators like GDP is crucial for analyzing India's economic performance.

items.title: Union Minister Piyush Goyal Inaugurates 'Smart India Hackathon 2026' in New Delhi
items.whyInNews: Union Minister for Commerce and Industry, Piyush Goyal, officially launched the 'Smart India Hackathon 2026' (SIH 2026) on April 17, 2026, at the Vigyan Bhavan in New Delhi, aiming to foster innovation among students.
items.summary: The highly anticipated 'Smart India Hackathon 2026', a nationwide initiative to promote innovation and problem-solving among students, was inaugurated by Union Minister Piyush Goyal on April 17, 2026. The event, held in New Delhi, brings together thousands of engineering and technology students to develop digital solutions for complex challenges faced by various industries and government organizations.
keyPoints: Smart India Hackathon 2026 inaugurated by Piyush Goyal.
Event held at Vigyan Bhavan, New Delhi.
Aims to foster innovation among students.
Focuses on developing digital solutions for industry and government.
importantPoints: Smart India Hackathon is an initiative by the Ministry of Education.
It is a nationwide, government-backed, open innovation model.
The first SIH was conducted in 2017.
The hackathon encourages students to work with industries and PSUs to solve their stated problems.
Piyush Goyal also holds the portfolio for Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution, and Textiles.
items.examRelevance: Science & Technology and General Awareness sections; awareness of national-level innovation initiatives and key ministries.

items.title: India Ranks 47th in the Global Innovation Index 2026, Switzerland Tops the List
items.whyInNews: The World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) released the Global Innovation Index (GII) 2026 on April 17, 2026, placing India at the 47th position out of 132 economies.
items.summary: In the latest Global Innovation Index 2026, India has improved its standing to 47th place, showcasing progress in its innovation ecosystem. Switzerland has once again secured the top spot globally. The index, published annually by WIPO, assesses innovation performance based on various indicators, including institutions, human capital and research, infrastructure, market sophistication, and business sophistication.
keyPoints: India ranked 47th in GII 2026.
Switzerland ranked 1st globally.
The index was released by WIPO on April 17, 2026.
India was ranked 52nd in the previous year's index.
importantPoints: Global Innovation Index (GII) is released by the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO).
WIPO is a specialized agency of the United Nations.
Its headquarters are in Geneva, Switzerland.
The GII ranks countries based on their innovation capabilities.
India's consistent improvement in the GII reflects its growing focus on research and development.
items.examRelevance: International Organizations, Reports & Indices, and General Awareness; understanding India's global standing in innovation.

items.title: ISRO Successfully Launches GSAT-32 Satellite for Enhanced Communication Services
items.whyInNews: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the communication satellite GSAT-32 from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Sriharikota on April 17, 2026, to bolster India's communication network.
items.summary: ISRO achieved another milestone on April 17, 2026, with the successful launch of its advanced communication satellite, GSAT-32. The launch took place from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota, Andhra Pradesh. GSAT-32 is designed to provide improved telecommunication, broadcasting, and disaster management communication services across the country.
keyPoints: ISRO launched GSAT-32 satellite.
Launch site: Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
Date of launch: April 17, 2026.
Satellite aims to enhance communication services.
importantPoints: ISRO is the space agency of the Government of India.
It was established on August 15, 1969.
Its headquarters are in Bengaluru, Karnataka.
GSAT series satellites are advanced communication satellites.
The launch vehicle used was likely a GSLV Mk III or similar.
items.examRelevance: Science & Technology and Defence; knowledge of India's space program and satellite launches is important.

items.title: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 5.15% in its April 2026 Monetary Policy Review
items.whyInNews: The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC), in its bi-monthly review on April 17, 2026, decided to keep the policy repo rate unchanged at 5.15% to support economic growth while ensuring inflation remains within the target band.
items.summary: On April 17, 2026, the RBI's MPC concluded its meeting and announced its decision to hold the repo rate steady at 5.15%. This decision aims to balance the need for continued economic stimulus with the objective of maintaining price stability. The reverse repo rate remains at 4.90%, and the bank rate at 5.40%.
keyPoints: Repo rate kept unchanged at 5.15%.
Decision made by RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Date of announcement: April 17, 2026.
Reverse repo rate stands at 4.90%.
importantPoints: The RBI is India's central bank, established on April 1, 1935.
Its headquarters are in Mumbai, Maharashtra.
The MPC is responsible for setting the policy repo rate.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
The inflation target for India is 4% with a tolerance band of +/- 2%.
items.examRelevance: Indian Economy section; understanding monetary policy tools like repo rate and the role of the RBI.

items.title: India and France Sign MoU for Enhanced Cooperation in Renewable Energy
items.whyInNews: India and France signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on April 17, 2026, in Paris, to strengthen their collaboration in the development and deployment of renewable energy technologies.
items.summary: A significant step towards bolstering clean energy initiatives, India and France inked an MoU on April 17, 2026, during a bilateral meeting in Paris. The agreement focuses on joint research, technology transfer, and investment in solar, wind, and green hydrogen projects, aiming to accelerate the transition to sustainable energy sources for both nations.
keyPoints: India and France signed an MoU on renewable energy.
Agreement signed on April 17, 2026.
Location of signing: Paris, France.
Focus areas include solar, wind, and green hydrogen.
importantPoints: France is a key partner for India in climate change and renewable energy initiatives.
The International Solar Alliance (ISA) was co-founded by India and France.
India has set ambitious renewable energy targets, aiming for 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
Green hydrogen is seen as a crucial fuel for the future, and India is promoting its production.
The MoU aims to facilitate technology exchange and joint ventures.
items.examRelevance: International Relations and Environment; understanding India's foreign policy and its commitment to climate action.

items.title: Defence Ministry Approves Procurement of Advanced Akash-NG Missile Systems Worth ₹8,500 Crore
items.whyInNews: The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, approved the procurement of 10 squadrons of the Akash-NG (New Generation) surface-to-air missile systems for the Indian Army and Air Force on April 17, 2026, at an estimated cost of ₹8,500 crore.
items.summary: In a significant boost to India's air defence capabilities, the Defence Ministry, on April 17, 2026, greenlit the purchase of advanced Akash-NG missile systems. The deal, valued at approximately ₹8,500 crore, includes 10 squadrons of these state-of-the-art missiles, which are designed to counter aerial threats at longer ranges and higher altitudes, enhancing the operational readiness of the Indian Army and Air Force.
keyPoints: Defence Ministry approved procurement of Akash-NG missile systems.
Deal value: ₹8,500 crore.
Number of squadrons: 10.
Chaired by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh.
importantPoints: Akash-NG is an upgraded version of the Akash missile system.
It has enhanced range and accuracy compared to its predecessor.
The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body for military procurements.
The Defence Minister is the chairman of the DAC.
Indigenous defence manufacturing is a key focus under the 'Make in India' initiative.
items.examRelevance: Defence and Security; knowledge of military procurements and indigenous defence capabilities.

items.title: Union Health Ministry Launches 'Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission 2.0' with Focus on Rural Areas
items.whyInNews: The Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched the second phase of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), named 'ABDM 2.0', on April 17, 2026, with a special emphasis on expanding its reach to rural and remote areas of the country.
items.summary: On April 17, 2026, the Union Health Ministry officially rolled out 'ABDM 2.0', the enhanced version of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission. This new phase aims to create a more robust and accessible digital health ecosystem, particularly by extending digital health records and services to underserved populations in rural India. The mission seeks to integrate healthcare services through digital means, improving patient care and administrative efficiency.
keyPoints: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission 2.0 launched.
Focus on expanding reach to rural areas.
Launched by the Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
Date: April 17, 2026.
importantPoints: ABDM aims to build the necessary backbone for a digital health ecosystem.
It provides a unique Health ID to citizens.
The first phase of ABDM was launched on September 27, 2021.
The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare is headed by Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya (as of current knowledge, subject to change).
Digital health records can improve continuity of care and reduce medical errors.
items.examRelevance: Social Issues and Health; understanding government initiatives related to healthcare and digital transformation.

items.title: Indian Railways to Introduce 'Vande Bharat Sleeper' Trains by December 2026
items.whyInNews: Indian Railways announced on April 17, 2026, that the first prototype of the Vande Bharat Sleeper train is expected to be ready by December 2026, aiming to provide a more comfortable and faster overnight travel experience for passengers.
items.summary: In a significant upgrade to its long-distance services, Indian Railways revealed on April 17, 2026, that the Vande Bharat Sleeper train, designed for overnight journeys, will see its first prototype ready by December 2026. These trains will feature modern amenities and improved sleeping arrangements, aiming to compete with premium train services and enhance passenger comfort on inter-state routes.
keyPoints: Vande Bharat Sleeper train prototype by December 2026.
Announced by Indian Railways.
Focus on overnight travel comfort.
Aims to upgrade long-distance train services.
importantPoints: Vande Bharat trains are semi-high-speed, indigenous trains.
The first Vande Bharat Express was flagged off on February 15, 2019.
Indian Railways is the world's largest railway network under a single management.
It operates under the Ministry of Railways.
The introduction of sleeper variants aims to cater to the demand for comfortable long-haul travel.
items.examRelevance: General Awareness and Transport; knowledge of Indian Railways' modernization plans and new train services.

items.title: Supreme Court Declines Urgent Hearing on Electoral Bonds Case, Lists for May 2026
items.whyInNews: The Supreme Court of India, on April 17, 2026, decided not to grant an urgent hearing to the petitions challenging the constitutionality of electoral bonds, scheduling the next hearing for May 2026.
items.summary: On April 17, 2026, a bench of the Supreme Court stated that the crucial case concerning the validity of electoral bonds will be heard in May 2026. The court was hearing multiple petitions seeking transparency in political party funding and challenging the electoral bond scheme introduced by the government in 2018. The delay in hearing has drawn attention from various political and civil society groups.
keyPoints: Supreme Court deferred hearing on Electoral Bonds case.
Next hearing scheduled for May 2026.
Petitions challenge the constitutionality of electoral bonds.
Electoral bond scheme was introduced in 2018.
importantPoints: Electoral bonds were introduced via the Finance Act, 2017.
The scheme aims to increase transparency in political funding.
Critics argue it benefits ruling parties and lacks true transparency.
The Supreme Court of India is the apex judicial body in India.
Article 131 of the Constitution deals with the original jurisdiction of the Supreme Court.
items.examRelevance: Polity and Indian Constitution; understanding the judicial process and issues related to electoral reforms.

items.title: India's Renewable Energy Capacity Surpasses 200 GW Milestone
items.whyInNews: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) announced on April 17, 2026, that India's total installed renewable energy capacity, excluding hydropower, has crossed the 200 Gigawatt (GW) mark.
items.summary: India has achieved a significant milestone in its clean energy journey, with its total installed renewable energy capacity exceeding 200 GW as of April 17, 2026. This includes contributions from solar, wind, biomass, and small hydro projects. The achievement underscores India's commitment to reducing its carbon footprint and increasing the share of non-fossil fuels in its energy mix.
keyPoints: India's renewable energy capacity crossed 200 GW.
Excludes large hydropower projects.
Announced by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
Date: April 17, 2026.
importantPoints: India aims for 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
Solar energy constitutes the largest share of renewable capacity.
The National Solar Mission was launched in 2010.
MNRE is the nodal ministry for all matters relating to new and renewable energy.
This milestone is crucial for India's climate commitments under the Paris Agreement.
items.examRelevance: Environment and Economy; understanding India's progress in renewable energy and climate change mitigation.

items.title: World Athletics Championships 2026: Neeraj Chopra Qualifies for Javelin Throw Finals
items.whyInNews: Indian javelin thrower Neeraj Chopra secured a spot in the finals of the World Athletics Championships 2026, held in Eugene, Oregon, USA, with a throw of 88.50 meters in the qualification round on April 17, 2026.
items.summary: Star Indian athlete Neeraj Chopra continued his impressive form at the World Athletics Championships 2026 in Eugene, USA, on April 17, 2026. He qualified for the men's javelin throw final with a remarkable throw of 88.50 meters in his first attempt, positioning himself as a strong contender for a medal.
keyPoints: Neeraj Chopra qualified for World Athletics Championships 2026 final.
Event venue: Eugene, Oregon, USA.
Qualifying throw: 88.50 meters.
Date: April 17, 2026.
importantPoints: Neeraj Chopra won India's first-ever Olympic gold medal in athletics at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics (held in 2021).
He won the gold medal in the javelin throw at the 2018 Asian Games.
The World Athletics Championships are held biennially.
This event is a major competition in track and field athletics.
Chopra's performance is a significant achievement for Indian athletics.
items.examRelevance: Sports and Awards; knowledge of major international sporting events and achievements of Indian athletes.

items.title: Government Launches National Logistics Policy 2.0 to Reduce Costs by 5%
items.whyInNews: The Union Cabinet approved the National Logistics Policy 2.0 on April 17, 2026, aiming to reduce logistics costs from the current 13-14% of GDP to below 10% within the next five years.
items.summary: On April 17, 2026, the Union Cabinet gave its nod to the National Logistics Policy 2.0, a comprehensive framework designed to streamline India's logistics sector. The policy aims to improve efficiency, reduce transportation costs by an estimated 5%, and enhance India's global ranking in logistics performance. Key focus areas include infrastructure development, technology adoption, and skill enhancement.
keyPoints: National Logistics Policy 2.0 approved.
Aims to reduce logistics costs to below 10% of GDP.
Target reduction: 5% within five years.
Approved by the Union Cabinet on April 17, 2026.
importantPoints: The first National Logistics Policy was launched in 2022.
Logistics costs in India are currently estimated at 13-14% of GDP.
Efficient logistics are crucial for economic growth and competitiveness.
The policy aims to integrate various modes of transport.
This initiative aligns with India's goal of becoming a global manufacturing hub.
items.examRelevance: Economy and Governance; understanding policies aimed at improving India's economic infrastructure and competitiveness.

items.title: PM Modi Inaugurates India's First Underwater Metro Tunnel in Kolkata
items.whyInNews: Prime Minister Narendra Modi virtually inaugurated India's first underwater metro tunnel, part of the Kolkata Metro's East-West corridor, on April 17, 2026, marking a significant engineering feat.
items.summary: A historic moment for Indian urban infrastructure, Prime Minister Narendra Modi on April 17, 2026, inaugurated the much-awaited underwater metro tunnel in Kolkata. The 4.8 km stretch runs under the Hooghly River, connecting Sector V station in Salt Lake to Howrah Maidan. This project is expected to significantly improve connectivity and reduce travel time in the metropolitan region.
keyPoints: India's first underwater metro tunnel inaugurated.
Location: Kolkata Metro, East-West corridor.
Runs under the Hooghly River.
Inaugurated by PM Narendra Modi on April 17, 2026.
importantPoints: The tunnel is approximately 520 meters long under the river.
The project is funded by the Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA).
The total cost of the East-West Metro corridor project is estimated to be around ₹8,600 crore.
Kolkata Metro is the oldest underground metro railway in India, commissioned in 1984.
This project showcases advanced tunneling technology in challenging geological conditions.
items.examRelevance: General Awareness, Infrastructure, and Geography; knowledge of major infrastructure projects and their significance.

items.title: India's First 'Dark Sky Reserve' Established in Ladakh
items.whyInNews: The Department of Science and Technology announced on April 17, 2026, the establishment of India's first 'Dark Sky Reserve' in Hanle, Ladakh, to promote astro-tourism and protect the night sky from light pollution.
items.summary: In a move to preserve its pristine night skies and boost tourism, India has established its first Dark Sky Reserve in Hanle village, Ladakh, on April 17, 2026. This initiative, supported by the Department of Science and Technology, aims to create a sanctuary for astronomical observation by minimizing artificial light pollution in the region, which is known for its clear skies and high altitude.
keyPoints: India's first Dark Sky Reserve established.
Location: Hanle, Ladakh.
Aims to promote astro-tourism.
Announced on April 17, 2026.
importantPoints: Hanle is home to the Indian Astronomical Observatory (IAO).
The reserve covers an area of approximately 1,091 square kilometers.
Dark Sky Reserves are designated areas that have policies in place to protect the night sky.
This initiative is expected to attract astronomers and tourists interested in stargazing.
Ladakh is a Union Territory known for its unique geography and culture.
items.examRelevance: Environment, Geography, and Tourism; understanding conservation efforts and unique geographical locations in India.

items.title: National Maritime Heritage Complex Project Gets Boost with ₹500 Crore Funding
items.whyInNews: The Union Cabinet, in its meeting on April 17, 2026, approved an additional funding of ₹500 crore for the development of the National Maritime Heritage Complex (NMHC) at Lothal, Gujarat.
items.summary: The ambitious National Maritime Heritage Complex project at Lothal, Gujarat, received a significant financial push on April 17, 2026, with the Union Cabinet approving an additional ₹500 crore. This funding will accelerate the development of the complex, which aims to showcase India's rich maritime history and heritage, including the ancient Indus Valley Civilization port city of Lothal.
keyPoints: Additional funding of ₹500 crore for NMHC.
Project location: Lothal, Gujarat.
Approved by the Union Cabinet on April 17, 2026.
Aims to showcase India's maritime history.
importantPoints: Lothal was one of the major port cities of the ancient Indus Valley Civilization.
The NMHC project is being developed by the Ministry of Culture.
It aims to be a world-class museum and research center.
The project is part of the broader 'Azadi Ka Amrit Mahotsav' celebrations.
This initiative highlights the importance of preserving India's historical and cultural heritage.
items.examRelevance: History, Culture, and Governance; understanding government projects related to heritage preservation and national development.

items.title: India Successfully Tests Indigenous Hypersonic Missile 'Pravaah'
items.whyInNews: The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) successfully conducted the maiden test flight of its indigenously developed hypersonic missile, 'Pravaah', from the Integrated Test Range in Chandipur, Odisha, on April 17, 2026.
items.summary: DRDO achieved a major breakthrough in missile technology on April 17, 2026, with the successful test of 'Pravaah', India's first indigenous hypersonic missile. The missile, capable of traveling at speeds exceeding Mach 5, was launched from Chandipur, Odisha, and met all mission objectives. This development significantly enhances India's strategic defence capabilities.
keyPoints: India tested indigenous hypersonic missile 'Pravaah'.
Test conducted by DRDO.
Launch site: Integrated Test Range, Chandipur, Odisha.
Missile speed: Exceeds Mach 5.
importantPoints: Hypersonic missiles travel at speeds of Mach 5 or higher (five times the speed of sound).
DRDO is the research and development arm of the Ministry of Defence.
It was established in 1958.
Its headquarters are in New Delhi.
The development of such advanced missile technology is crucial for national security.
items.examRelevance: Science & Technology and Defence; understanding India's advancements in missile technology and defence research.

items.title: World Bank Projects India's Economic Growth at 7.5% for FY 2026-27
items.whyInNews: The World Bank, in its latest 'Global Economic Prospects' report released on April 17, 2026, projected India's GDP growth rate to be 7.5% for the fiscal year 2026-27.
items.summary: The World Bank has provided an optimistic outlook for the Indian economy, forecasting a growth rate of 7.5% for the fiscal year 2026-27 in its report published on April 17, 2026. The projection is attributed to robust domestic demand, increased investment, and the government's reform measures. The report also noted that global economic growth is expected to moderate.
keyPoints: World Bank projects India's GDP growth at 7.5% for FY 2026-27.
Report released on April 17, 2026.
Reason cited: Strong domestic demand and investment.
Global Economic Prospects report.
importantPoints: The World Bank is an international financial institution that provides loans and grants to the governments of low- and middle-income countries for the purpose of pursuing capital projects.
It was established in 1944.
Its headquarters are in Washington, D.C., USA.
The World Bank's forecasts are closely watched by policymakers and investors.
India is one of the fastest-growing major economies globally.
items.examRelevance: Economy and International Organizations; understanding global economic forecasts and India's position in the global economy.

items.title: National Panchayati Raj Day Celebrated on April 24, 2026
items.whyInNews: While the main celebration is on April 24, 2026, preparations and related announcements are being made, highlighting the significance of local self-governance in India.
items.summary: The nation gears up to celebrate National Panchayati Raj Day on April 24, 2026, a day dedicated to recognizing the efforts of Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) in rural governance and development. This day commemorates the passing of the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act in 1992, which institutionalized Panchayati Raj in India, empowering local self-governance at the village level.
keyPoints: National Panchayati Raj Day is celebrated on April 24.
Commemorates the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992.
Focuses on empowering local self-governance.
Recognizes the role of Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs).
importantPoints: The 73rd Amendment Act came into effect on April 24, 1993.
It added Part IX to the Constitution, dealing with the Panchayats.
PRIs are the third tier of government in rural India.
The Ministry of Panchayati Raj is responsible for promoting the development of Panchayati Raj Institutions.
This day emphasizes the importance of decentralized governance and citizen participation.
items.examRelevance: Polity and Constitution; understanding the structure of local governance in India and constitutional amendments.
